# terrible_junipernetworks_junos_junos_facts (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `available_network_resources` (Boolean) When 'True' a list of network resources for which resource modules are available will be provided.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `config_format` (String) The *config_format* argument specifies the format of the configuration when serializing output from the device. This argument is applicable only when `config` value is present in *gather_subset*. The *config_format* should be supported by the junos version running on device. This value is not applicable while fetching old style facts that is when `ofacts` value is present in value if *gather_subset* value. This option is valid only for `gather_subset` values.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `gather_network_resources` (String) When supplied, this argument will restrict the facts collected to a given subset. Possible values for this argument include all and the resources like interfaces, vlans etc. Can specify a list of values to include a larger subset. Values can also be used with an initial `!` to specify that a specific subset should not be collected. Valid subsets are 'all', 'interfaces', 'lacp', 'lacp_interfaces', 'lag_interfaces', 'l2_interfaces', 'l3_interfaces', 'lldp_global', 'lldp_interfaces', 'vlans'.
- `gather_subset` (String) When supplied, this argument will restrict the facts collected to a given subset.  Possible values for this argument include `all`, `hardware`, `config`, `interfaces` and `min`. Can specify a list of values to include a larger subset.  Values can also be used with an initial `!` to specify that a specific subset should not be collected. To maintain backward compatibility old style facts can be retrieved by explicitly adding `ofacts`  to value, this requires junos-eznc to be installed as a prerequisite. Valid value of gather_subset are default, hardware, config, interfaces, ofacts. If `ofacts` is present in the list it fetches the old style facts (fact keys without 'ansible_' prefix) and it requires junos-eznc library to be installed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `ansible_facts` (String) Returns the facts collect from the device
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
